name: cf-session

criteria.name: session_saved_correctly
criteria.description: Session state is captured completely and accurately when saving

scoring.0: Save fails or produces an empty/corrupt file
scoring.1: Session saved but missing critical context (current task, modified files, etc.)
scoring.2: Session saved with main context but some details lost (e.g., partial file list)
scoring.3: Session saved with complete context: current task, modified files, decisions made, and next steps

criteria.name: session_loaded_correctly
criteria.description: Loading a session restores enough context to continue work without re-explaining

scoring.0: Load fails or restored context is unusable
scoring.1: Session loads but requires significant re-explanation of the task
scoring.2: Session loads with main context intact but some details need clarification
scoring.3: Session loads seamlessly — can continue work exactly where it left off

criteria.name: format_valid
criteria.description: Session file uses valid format and is parseable

scoring.0: File is not valid YAML/JSON — parse errors
scoring.1: File parses but has structural issues (missing required fields)
scoring.2: File parses correctly and has required fields but some optional fields missing
scoring.3: File is valid, well-structured, and includes all expected fields

notes: When evaluating load, the test should save a session, start a new conversation, load it, and verify the agent can continue the task. Session files should be human-readable.
